decision: Keep the opt-in TorchTitan TileLang compressed-dKV bridge as the semantic integration path. The vectorized CPU route-plan rewrite removes the immediate Python-loop cliff, but the path is still not a production speed promotion because S4096 bridge timing is dominated by explicit selected-state/probability/logit construction plus remaining CPU route metadata.

implementation_change.panelize_groups: vectorized per-panel cumsum/scatter instead of token/selected Python loops
implementation_change.compact_slots: dense route-tile by group-panel active mask plus cumsum slot lookup for panelized LI routes
implementation_change.verifier: adds timing-only flags --skip-parity and --skip-explicit-timing for large S4096 bridge timing

read.what_improved: The route metadata bridge is no longer hundreds of ms at S512 and the target-shape CPU route-plan wall fell by about 11.9x.
read.what_did_not_improve: The selected-limit TileLang dKV kernel itself remains about 6 ms, but the integrated S4096 backward bridge is still about 75.7 ms because it materializes selected KV/probs/grad_logits and computes route metadata on CPU.
read.production_requirement: Reuse or GPU-produce LI route metadata, avoid selected-KV materialization, and fuse or adjacent-produce P/dP so the TileLang dKV producer consumes on-chip tiles rather than materialized [S,K,H] slabs.

next: Move route selected-limit metadata construction onto GPU or reuse the existing cheap LI prepack output.
Split S4096 explicit_tilelang_dkv timing into selected-KV gather, score/prob/logit construction, local dKV, route plan, pack, and TileLang kernel.
Prototype a no-selected-KV bridge that feeds compressed route/P/dP directly into the selected-limit producer.
